title: One Punch Banta

jokes.text: And Zelinko, you got cab 636, and wash it!
jokes.setup: Louie dispatching cabs to drivers
jokes.punchline: Single out one driver for extra work/humiliation
jokes.explanation: Establishes Louie's character as petty and controlling. Simple but effective character beat.

dialogue.speaker: Louie
dialogue.line: Hey! You! Yeah, you in the yellow cab. I got a special job for you. It's gonna be hot, it's gonna be sweaty, and nobody else wants it.

dialogue.speaker: Driver
dialogue.line: What is it, Louie?

dialogue.speaker: Louie
dialogue.line: I need you to go pick up a guy from the bus station, take him to the airport, then come back here and wash my car. And if you complain, I'll give the next three fares to Nardo.

jokes.text: It hurts me to see you not working. It makes me want to press a nerve in your neck.
jokes.setup: Louie expressing fake concern for Latka
jokes.punchline: Reveals violent impulse disguised as caring
jokes.explanation: Perfect Louie line - fake sentiment masking cruelty. Well-structured misdirection.

dialogue.speaker: Louie
dialogue.line: Latka, I'm worried about you. You been working too hard lately.

dialogue.speaker: Latka
dialogue.line: Thank you, Louie. That's very kind.

dialogue.speaker: Louie
dialogue.line: Yeah, you need to take it easy. In fact, why don't you go home early today? Get some rest.

dialogue.speaker: Latka
dialogue.line: Really? You mean it?

dialogue.speaker: Louie
dialogue.line: Of course I mean it. I'd hate to see something happen to you. So just get out of here before I lose my temper and break your legs.

jokes.text: Because you are a terrible person.
jokes.setup: Latka asks why Louie hit him
jokes.punchline: Brutally honest, matter-of-fact explanation
jokes.explanation: Peak Louie - maximum cruelty with minimum words. Perfect character integration.

dialogue.speaker: Latka
dialogue.line: Why you hit me, Louie?

dialogue.speaker: Louie
dialogue.line: Because you're an easy target and it makes me feel better about my miserable life.

jokes.text: The escalating booking claims: $120 to $110 to $100 to $90 to $80
jokes.setup: Tony claiming he booked $120
jokes.punchline: Each statement reveals he booked progressively less
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation structure showing Tony's inability to maintain a lie. Well-crafted reveal.

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: I booked $120.

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.line: That's great!

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: Well, actually it was $80.

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.line: Oh.

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: Okay, it was $40.

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.line: Tony...

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: Alright, $20.

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.line: And?

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: They gave me cab fare home.

jokes.text: Haul your sweaty carcass over here this second. Did you detect mellowing?
jokes.setup: Louie rudely summoning Alex
jokes.punchline: Asking if Alex noticed him becoming nicer
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic contrast between Louie's behavior and his self-perception. Sharp writing.

dialogue.speaker: Louie
dialogue.line: Hey, Alex! Get over here!

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.line: What is it, Louie?

dialogue.speaker: Louie
dialogue.line: Have you noticed I've been nicer lately?

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.line: Nicer? Louie, you just yelled at me.

dialogue.speaker: Louie
dialogue.line: Yeah, but did you hear the tone? I didn't call you stupid.

jokes.text: You're through, loser. That's the nice way.
jokes.setup: Louie offering to fire John nicely
jokes.punchline: Calling someone a loser is the 'nice' option
jokes.explanation: Shows Louie's warped sense of kindness. Good character consistency.

dialogue.speaker: Louie
dialogue.line: I'll fire you nice, John. I'll call you a loser.

jokes.text: Whoo! A threat. I heard a threat... Ooh, a threat with an explanation... A threat that worked.
jokes.setup: Alex threatening to quit if John is fired
jokes.punchline: Louie's play-by-play commentary on the threat
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalating commentary structure. Louie's detached analysis of his own defeat is brilliant.

dialogue.speaker: Louie
dialogue.line: He's gonna quit. He's walking toward the door. He's got his hand on the doorknob. He's turning it. He's opening the door. He's walking through. He's closing the door. He's gone.

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.line: I'm back.

dialogue.speaker: Louie
dialogue.line: He's back. He's walking toward me. He's got a very angry look on his face.

jokes.text: Your gym called... That information will cost you another buck... They didn't say nothing.
jokes.setup: Charging Tony for increasingly specific information
jokes.punchline: The expensive information is that there was no information
jokes.explanation: Perfect scam structure showing Louie's opportunism. Well-executed bait and switch.

dialogue.speaker: Louie
dialogue.line: That'll be five bucks.

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: Five bucks? For what?

dialogue.speaker: Louie
dialogue.line: For telling you something.

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: What'd you tell me?

dialogue.speaker: Louie
dialogue.line: That'll be another five bucks if you want to know what I told you.

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: This is ridiculous.

dialogue.speaker: Louie
dialogue.line: Ten bucks says you think so.

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: Okay, okay. What was the information?

dialogue.speaker: Louie
dialogue.line: That'll be fifteen bucks.

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: Fifteen?!

dialogue.speaker: Louie
dialogue.line: You want it specific, it costs more. There was no information.

jokes.text: Never pick up a cripple... You call them 'handicapped persons'... The people you should never pick up are handicapped persons.
jokes.setup: Alex correcting Louie's offensive language
jokes.punchline: Louie uses the 'correct' term for the same awful advice
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of political correctness. Shows Louie missing the point entirely while technically complying. Masterful misdirection.

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.line: Louie, you can't say that. The correct term is...

dialogue.speaker: Louie
dialogue.line: Yeah, yeah, I got it. So what I meant to say is...

jokes.text: You have to forgive Louie. He's himself today.
jokes.setup: Standard 'not himself today' excuse
jokes.punchline: Twisting it to mean his awful behavior is normal
jokes.explanation: Clever inversion of a common phrase. Maximum humor from minimal words.

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.line: I'm not myself today.

dialogue.speaker: Bobby
dialogue.line: Yeah, well, whoever you are, you're a real jerk.

jokes.text: Imagine, the champ hitting my face.
jokes.setup: Tony's excitement about sparring with the champ
jokes.punchline: Focuses on getting beaten up as the exciting part
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Tony's innocent excitement about his own potential beating. Good character work.

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: I'm gonna spar with the champ! Can you believe it? Me and the champ, in the ring together!

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: He's gonna punch me, and I'm gonna punch him back. Well, I'm gonna try to punch him back. Mostly he's just gonna punch me.

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: But that's the exciting part - getting beaten up by the champ!

jokes.text: He can wear it when he's fighting and when he's convalescing.
jokes.setup: Discussing the practical value of buying Tony a robe
jokes.punchline: Assumes he'll need it while recovering from injuries
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about Tony's inevitable defeat. Solid structure but not character-specific.

dialogue.speaker: Bobby
dialogue.line: You're buying Tony a robe? What for?

dialogue.speaker: Bobby
dialogue.line: Oh, I see. You assume he'll need it while recovering from injuries.

jokes.text: It's from everybody down at the garage except for, uh, Louie, Vince, Charlie, and Ed. They refused to cough up.
jokes.setup: Presenting Tony with a gift from everyone
jokes.punchline: Listing everyone who didn't contribute
jokes.explanation: Honest but tactless gift presentation. Functional humor but nothing special.

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.line: Tony, we all got together and got you a little something.

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: Oh wow, that's great! Who contributed?

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.line: Well, there's me, Jim, Marty, Elaine, Bobby, Carol, and Reverend Jim.

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: That's wonderful. Who didn't chip in?

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.line: Louie.

jokes.text: Once, the announcer said: 'In this corner, Holiday Inn.'
jokes.setup: Tony explaining he only had a towel before
jokes.punchline: Mistaken for a hotel advertisement due to the towel
jokes.explanation: Clever visual gag about Tony's low-rent boxing career. Good structure and specificity.

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: I only had a towel before.

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: Someone thought I was a hotel advertisement.

jokes.text: I like 'son.'
jokes.setup: Manager asking what Tony wants to be called
jokes.punchline: Tony prefers the condescending term
jokes.explanation: Shows Tony's innocent acceptance of condescension. Good character moment.

dialogue.speaker: Manager
dialogue.line: What would you like to be called?

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: How about 'Reverend'? I'm a cab driver, but I'd like to be called 'Reverend.'

dialogue.speaker: Manager
dialogue.line: Reverend? Why?

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: Because I'd rather be called 'Reverend' than what everybody else calls me.

dialogue.speaker: Manager
dialogue.line: What do they call you?

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: A cab driver.

jokes.text: All the ones with big sleeves had Japanese paintings on them.
jokes.setup: Tony struggling to get robe off over boxing gloves
jokes.punchline: Specific reason for choosing impractical robe
jokes.explanation: Mildly funny specificity but not particularly clever or character-driven.

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: I can't get this robe off! These boxing gloves won't let me!

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.line: Tony, why did you wear that robe in the first place if you knew you'd have trouble taking it off?

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: Because it makes me look like a real boxer!

jokes.text: Yeah, it's the same with sex. You get better? They do.
jokes.setup: Discussion about performing better with better partners
jokes.punchline: Bobby admits he doesn't improve, his partners do
jokes.explanation: Sharp self-deprecating humor with perfect economy. Good timing and character work.

dialogue.speaker: Bobby
dialogue.line: I don't improve, my partners do.

jokes.text: Champ, I'm sorry I hit you so hard.
jokes.setup: Tony apparently knocks down the champion
jokes.punchline: Apologizes for his success
jokes.explanation: Pure Tony character moment - apologizing for doing well. Sets up later revelation.

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: I'm sorry, I didn't mean to knock him down. I was just trying to... I mean, I wasn't expecting to actually...

jokes.text: Word not here.
jokes.setup: Latka describing his romantic encounter to Louie
jokes.punchline: Can't find the English word for sex, gestures instead
jokes.explanation: Typical Latka language barrier humor with physical comedy element.

dialogue.speaker: Latka
dialogue.line: So I tell her, 'You want to...?' and I do this with my hands.

dialogue.speaker: Louie
dialogue.line: What'd she say?

dialogue.speaker: Latka
dialogue.line: She say, 'Yes, yes!' So we go to her apartment and we... we do the thing with the... you know.

dialogue.speaker: Louie
dialogue.line: The thing?

dialogue.speaker: Latka
dialogue.line: The... the... I cannot find the words in English. Is like this... and then this... and then we both make the noise like 'Uhhhhh!'

jokes.text: Always remember, lust is better than love.
jokes.setup: Louie correcting Latka about love vs. lust
jokes.punchline: Cynical life philosophy delivered as wisdom
jokes.explanation: Perfect Louie wisdom - cynical but delivered with authority. Good character consistency.

dialogue.speaker: Louie
dialogue.line: Listen, Latka, there's a difference between love and lust. Love is when you care about someone's feelings, their happiness, their future. Lust is just physical attraction.

dialogue.speaker: Latka
dialogue.line: So which one do I feel?

dialogue.speaker: Louie
dialogue.line: Kid, if you gotta ask, it ain't love. It's lust. And if it lasts more than a week, it's indigestion.

jokes.text: I in lust. What else is new?
jokes.setup: Latka proudly announcing his new emotional state
jokes.punchline: Alex's weary response suggests this is routine
jokes.explanation: Mild callback humor showing this is typical Latka behavior.

dialogue.speaker: Latka
dialogue.line: I am happy! I am sad! I am angry! I am confused!

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.line: Yeah, it's only Tuesday.

jokes.text: I think it's the spray cans.
jokes.setup: Louie complaining about the world going crazy
jokes.punchline: Blames aerosol cans for societal problems
jokes.explanation: Random but perfectly Louie explanation for world problems. Good absurdist humor.

dialogue.speaker: Louie
dialogue.line: The world's going crazy, I tell ya. Everybody's nuts. You know what's responsible for all this? Aerosol cans. That's right, aerosol cans. They're destroying the ozone layer, making people insane. I read it somewhere.

jokes.text: Buy a new cup.
jokes.setup: Asked how he'll celebrate his big fight
jokes.punchline: Plans to buy basic protective equipment
jokes.explanation: Perfect understated response showing Tony's modest means and practical mindset.

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: I'm gonna go out and buy myself a nice pair of headgear, a mouthpiece, and some good boxing gloves.

jokes.text: He's so good you can look up his record.
jokes.setup: Tony asking how good his opponent is
jokes.punchline: Being recordable means you're legitimate
jokes.explanation: Clever observation about boxing legitimacy. Good structure and insight.

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: How good is this guy?

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.line: Well, he's recordable.

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: Recordable?

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.line: Yeah, if you're good enough to be recorded, you're legitimate.

jokes.text: Whoo, Mr. Big Booker bets five whole dollars on his friend who he's sure is going to win.
jokes.setup: Alex making a small bet on Tony
jokes.punchline: Sarcastic commentary on the tiny bet size
jokes.explanation: Typical Louie needling, pointing out the contradiction between confidence and bet size.

dialogue.speaker: Louie
dialogue.line: A dollar? A dollar?! You're betting a dollar on Tony? What are you gonna do with your winnings, buy yourself a cup of coffee?

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.line: Louie, it's not about the money.

dialogue.speaker: Louie
dialogue.line: Not about the money? Kid, with you it's never about the money. It's about the principle. And the principle here is you're a cheapskate.

jokes.text: You're going to give up a Friday night... The busiest night of the week... To go watch the macaroni pony fight?
jokes.setup: Louie's disbelief that drivers will attend Tony's fight
jokes.punchline: Ethnic slur disguised as creative nickname
jokes.explanation: Louie's casual cruelty expressed through creative insults. Character-appropriate but dated.

jokes.text: Nobody.
jokes.setup: Louie expecting the garage to be full on Friday night
jokes.punchline: Completely empty garage - everyone went to Tony's fight
jokes.explanation: Perfect visual payoff to Louie's cynical prediction. Shows friendship trumps money.

dialogue.speaker: Louie
dialogue.line: Where is everybody? It's Friday night, the garage should be packed!

dialogue.speaker: Louie
dialogue.line: They all went to Tony's fight.

jokes.text: Always wanted to do that.
jokes.setup: Louie alone in the empty garage
jokes.punchline: Admits he's always wanted to sing/perform
jokes.explanation: Rare vulnerable moment for Louie. Shows his hidden humanity beneath the cruelty.

dialogue.speaker: Louie De Palma
dialogue.line: You know, I've always wanted to sing. To perform. To get up on stage and really... really belt it out.

jokes.text: You ever try to listen to the Olympics on the radio?
jokes.setup: Tony explaining he missed the Olympics because his TV broke
jokes.punchline: Absurdity of experiencing visual events through audio
jokes.explanation: Clever observational humor about media formats. Could work for any character but fits Tony's earnestness.

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: My TV broke during the Olympics. I had to listen to it on the radio.

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: Do you know how hard it is to watch the gymnastics on radio?

jokes.text: I like the theater better. They just say 'five minutes.'
jokes.setup: Bobby commenting on boxing's graphic pre-fight warnings
jokes.punchline: Prefers theater's genteel approach
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Bobby's theatrical background and preference for refinement.

dialogue.speaker: Bobby Benson
dialogue.line: You know, I hate those graphic warnings they put on boxing matches. 'WARNING: This event may contain graphic violence.' I mean, come on, we know what we're getting into.

dialogue.speaker: Bobby Benson
dialogue.line: You know what I like? Theater. You go to a play, they don't warn you about anything. You just sit down and enjoy some culture.

jokes.text: I like the theater better. They just say 'five minutes.'
jokes.setup: Comparing fight preparation to theater
jokes.punchline: Theater's more civilized approach to timing
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bobby - bringing his acting background into any situation. Good character specificity.

dialogue.speaker: Bobby
dialogue.line: In the theater, they say 'places everyone' before the curtain goes up. In a fight, nobody says that. You just get socked.

jokes.text: You didn't knock down the champ.
jokes.setup: Tony's pride in knocking down the champion
jokes.punchline: Manager reveals it was fake
jokes.explanation: Major plot revelation that recontextualizes earlier events. Strong story beat.

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: That was amazing, Tony. You knocked down the champion!

dialogue.speaker: Louie
dialogue.line: Yeah, kid. That was something special.

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: I told you I had it in me!

dialogue.speaker: Louie
dialogue.line: Tony, I gotta tell you something. That guy wasn't the real champion.

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: What do you mean he wasn't the real champion?

dialogue.speaker: Louie
dialogue.line: He was a fake. We hired him to take a dive.

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: You mean... I didn't actually knock down the champion?

dialogue.speaker: Louie
dialogue.line: That's right, kid.

jokes.text: You're sure you don't mind?
jokes.setup: Tony threatening to punch the champion
jokes.punchline: Politely asks permission before violence
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tony character moment - maintaining politeness even while threatening violence. Excellent contrast.

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: Hey, excuse me, champ. Would you mind if I punched you in the face?

dialogue.speaker: Champion
dialogue.line: What? No!

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: Okay, thank you anyway.

jokes.text: Hey, don't you know enough not to interrupt a man at prayer?
jokes.setup: Fighter asking what's wrong with Tony
jokes.punchline: Reveals Tony is praying, not having a breakdown
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection about Tony's apparent distress. Respectful humor about prayer.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown fighter
dialogue.line: What's wrong with Tony? Is he having a breakdown?

dialogue.speaker: Tony Banta
dialogue.line: No, I'm praying.

jokes.text: Your opponent.
jokes.setup: Tony asking who this guy is
jokes.punchline: Casual introduction to the man about to beat him up
jokes.explanation: Nice understated moment. The casual delivery makes the situation more awkward.

dialogue.speaker: Tony Banta
dialogue.line: Who is this guy?

dialogue.speaker: Frankie Wallace
dialogue.line: Oh, this is my friend Vince. He's gonna beat you up.

jokes.text: Gimme.
jokes.setup: Alex insisting on paying his lost bet
jokes.punchline: Louie's instant greed overcomes his fake generosity
jokes.explanation: Perfect Louie moment - greed immediately trumps any pretense of nobility. Pure character consistency.

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.line: No, no, Louie. You won fair and square. I lost the bet, so I'm paying you.

dialogue.speaker: Louie
dialogue.line: Alex, Alex. Forget about it. What are friends for? Keep your money.

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.line: I insist, Louie. A bet's a bet.

dialogue.speaker: Louie
dialogue.line: How much was it?

jokes.text: And I'm going to be here... Making a fortune.
jokes.setup: Everyone pledging continued support for Tony
jokes.punchline: Louie's support is purely financial motivation
jokes.explanation: Classic Louie ending - puncturing the sentiment with pure self-interest. Good character consistency.

dialogue.speaker: Louie
dialogue.line: Don't worry, Tony. You got my support. I'm behind you one hundred percent.

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: Thanks, Louie. That means a lot.

dialogue.speaker: Louie
dialogue.line: Yeah, well, as long as you keep making me money, I'll be behind you two hundred percent.

jokes.text: And I'm going to be here... Making a fortune.
jokes.setup: Everyone pledging support for Tony's comeback
jokes.punchline: Louie's purely mercenary motivation
jokes.explanation: Good ending button showing Louie's consistent greed amid the warm friendship moment.

dialogue.speaker: Louie
dialogue.line: Tony, I'm behind you all the way. One hundred percent. You need anything, you just say the word.

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: Thanks, Louie. That means a lot.

dialogue.speaker: Louie
dialogue.line: Hey, forget about it. We're all rooting for you. Now, when you make your comeback and you're back on top, you're not gonna forget your old pal Louie, are ya?

tmdb_overview: Tony is ecstatic when a lucky punch of his brings a boxing champion to his knees.

guest_stars.name: Carlos Palomino
guest_stars.character: Welterweight Champion of the World Carlos Palomino

insight: Early Taxi balances character work with tight setups, landing 38 jokes across 24 minutes.
